title: Reduce the risk of cheating without lowering engagement
text:
Anti-Cheating

# Reduce the risk of cheating without lowering engagement

Our considered approach to anti-cheating means you’ll trust the results without having to view every assessment and candidates will remain engaged in the process.

Book a Demo

Vervoe anti-cheating dashboard showing plagiarism and location detection

Anti-cheating candidate selection screen

Our Approach

## Anti-cheating that serves hirers and candidates

When we looked at anti-cheating methods for Vervoe we had to holistically consider the impact of some features on a candidate’s ability to unbiasedly complete the assessment. Is authenticity more important than experience? Would losing great candidates due to privacy concerns be an outcome our customers were willing to accept?

We decided to take a considered position, rather than just developing a list of features, we aimed to find a balance that would complement our product.

Randomization

## Weed out the cheaters

Randomization is available to all customers across all assessments. In its simplest form it allows you to mix up the questions in an assessment so each candidate receives a slightly different set of questions. This minimizes the risk that information will be shared about the content between candidates. Randomization can be on a question or skill level, and some question types like multiple choice even allow randomization within the question itself.

Assessment randomization settings

### For Assessments

Randomize questions used in assessments to reduce the risk of cheating.

### For Multiple-Choice

Mix up the selection of multiple-choice answers shown to different candidates.

### For Skills

Weed out any copying by randomizing questions assessing individual skills.

90%

Faster Hiring Time

67%

Reduced Turnover

98%

Candidate Satisfaction

Geolocation detection dashboard

Geolocation Detection

## Inconsistent behaviour

We use geolocation detection to determine if a candidate completes an assessment from multiple locations. If the locations and the timestamps are incompatible the candidate will be flagged for you to review. Our method ensures that genuine candidates that happen to start an assessment in one location and complete it in another location are not disadvantaged by this process.

Plagiarism

## Detect duplicates

Our plagiarism detection is powered by our machine learning models so it’s constantly evolving and learning from what it finds. All candidate text responses are run through our plagiarism detector - if two or more candidates have the same sentence format for a particular question they’ll be flagged for your review. You’ll see the content highlighted that has been deemed plagiarism so you can review it in context.

You can also turn this feature off for “complete this sentence” type questions that test for English comprehension, or questions that you know will have the same response.

Plagiarism detection highlighting in Vervoe

Candidate completing a video assessment

Candidate Imagery

## Genuine candidates only

The use of video and audio questions gives you a real-time look at who is responding to the questions in the assessment if potential cheating has been flagged. Using imagery or voice match as the last step in detecting cheating ensures bias stays firmly out of your hiring process.

### The features we implement:

* Work for mobile users
* Encourage compliance, reward the right behavior, and give confidence to honest candidates
* Respect the privacy of candidates
* Create transparency on what is monitored
* Be consistent across all customers and plans
* Positively impact how candidates view the experience
* Flag cheating but also highlight authenticity

Immersive Questions

## Test skills in context

Our skills assessments use immersive question types that bring your role to life and help you engage and test the ability of your candidates. Imagine watching an enterprise sales associate give a live pitch from a deck they’ve just helped create, or a marketing analyst pull game-changing insights from data in a spreadsheet, or a UX designer optimize your onboarding flow in under an hour.

Immersive question types recreate the day-to-day tasks specific to your role so your candidates can shine - and cheating becomes far harder and less useful.

Candidate completing an immersive job simulation

Vervoe platform detecting AI-assisted cheating

Overcoming Generative AI

## ChatGPT and other AI tools

Vervoe’s immersive question types are best placed to minimize the effectiveness of candidates cheating using ChatGPT. Vervoe goes beyond text-based answers to simulate true day-in-the-life scenarios and test candidates’ application of their skills through tasks, rather than standalone questions that test recall knowledge.

Our anti-cheating features have been updated to combat AI-assisted plagiarism. We have integrated ChatGPT and web sources into our detection models to make flagging even more accurate - all without penalizing candidates who are able to leverage AI to enhance their work performance.

Our support team can recommend best practices on using Vervoe assessments to replicate real work environments, even with text-based questions, designed to measure skills in context rather than standalone recall.

## Want more information on how we mitigate cheating?

Head over to our help center for more in-depth information about our:

Advanced Anti-CheatingChatGPT and Other AI Tools

Vervoe's advanced anti-cheating solutions

## Explore more features

### Assessment Builder

Our AI-powered assessment builder guides you from job title to skills and questions with sample answers.

### Expert Library

Our growing library of ready-to-use assessments are written by leading I/O psychologists and industry professionals.

### Assessment Insight

Optimize your assessments with in-depth insights through reporting and analytics and make better hires.

## Common questions

How does Vervoe prevent cheating in assessments?

Vervoe uses a layered approach including question randomization, geolocation detection, ML-powered plagiarism detection, and immersive job-simulation question types that are difficult to cheat on even with AI tools like ChatGPT.

Can candidates cheat using ChatGPT on Vervoe assessments?

Vervoe's immersive question types simulate real day-in-the-life tasks — like building a spreadsheet model, creating a presentation, or solving a practical coding challenge — making them much harder to game with ChatGPT than text-only questions. Vervoe also integrates AI and web sources into its plagiarism detection.

Does Vervoe use invasive proctoring like webcam monitoring?

No. Vervoe takes a considered approach to anti-cheating that balances integrity with candidate privacy and experience. Features are designed to flag suspicious behaviour without penalizing genuine candidates.

What is geolocation detection in Vervoe?

Vervoe uses geolocation detection to identify if a candidate completes an assessment from multiple incompatible locations. If timestamps and locations don't align, the candidate is flagged for review.

## See Vervoe in action

Confidently test and evaluate candidates from anywhere in the world - with integrity built in from the start.
